# Competitor Intelligence Report — FactoryLM / CraneSync
**Date:** 2026-06-19 (auto-generated by scheduled task)  
**Scope:** 25 competitors and innovators across AI-powered industrial maintenance, CMMS, predictive maintenance, industrial copilots, and crane/heavy equipment maintenance.

---

## Your Business Context

**FactoryLM / MIRA** (factorylm.com) — AI-powered maintenance intelligence platform. Structures unorganized maintenance data (manuals in filing cabinets, mismatched PLC tags, tribal knowledge) into a "Maintenance Intelligence Namespace." MIRA AI copilot runs on top for grounded, cited diagnostic answers. Serve industrial/manufacturing plants via $500 assessment → $2–5K/mo pilot → $499/mo operating layer.

**CraneSync** (cranesync.com) — AI-powered field service assistant and real-time monitoring for industrial cranes. Two-component platform: crane condition monitoring system + AI-driven service management for service managers.

---

## 25 Competitors & Innovators

### CATEGORY 1 — Direct AI Maintenance Copilots (Closest Competitors)

---

**1. Augury**
- **Website:** [augury.com](https://www.augury.com)
- **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/company/augury-systems](https://www.linkedin.com/company/augury-systems)
- **What they do:** The established enterprise leader in industrial AI for uptime. Started with vibration/ultrasound machine health monitoring, now pitching a full "Industrial AI Workforce" — AI agents covering machine health, process health, and operations for every plant role.
- **Scale:** 170+ global manufacturers including DuPont, Colgate, Heineken, Frito-Lay, Shell. 1.1B+ hours of real machine readings. 40+ countries.
- **Funding:** $300M+ raised. Named a Leader in Verdantix Green Quadrant for Industrial AI Analytics 2025.
- **Why they matter to you:** They're the high-end benchmark. They charge enterprise prices for a full-service model with CAT III/IV vibration analysts bundled in. FactoryLM's differentiator is documentation-grounded answers + namespace structuring at accessible SMB pricing.
- **Key gap vs. FactoryLM:** No OEM documentation namespace. No PLC tag reconciliation. Focused on rotating equipment vibration, not multi-source maintenance intelligence.

---

**2. TRACTIAN**
- **Website:** [tractian.com](https://tractian.com/en)
- **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/company/get-tractian](https://www.linkedin.com/company/get-tractian/)
- **What they do:** End-to-end industrial operating system: industrial-grade IoT sensors (vibration, temperature, RPM) → cloud AI → CMMS (TracOS™) → OEE production tracking. Detects 70+ rotating equipment fault types.
- **Scale:** 1,500+ U.S. and global manufacturers (McKesson, Air Liquide, Whirlpool, Cummins, Verizon, CSX). Forbes AI 50 (World's Best AI Companies). SAP Silver Partner.
- **HQ:** Atlanta, GA | **Founded:** 2019
- **Funding:** ~$45M raised (TechCrunch Series A $15M 2022, growth since).
- **Why they matter:** Strong sensor hardware + CMMS + OEE in one platform — a "full stack" play. G2 Spring 2026 Leader. Very competitive price-sensitive mid-market.
- **Key gap vs. FactoryLM:** Hardware-dependent (sensors required). No manual/documentation namespace or PLC tag reconciliation. Rotating equipment focus only, not multi-asset knowledge management.

---

**3. Factory AI (f7i.ai)**
- **Website:** [f7i.ai](https://f7i.ai)
- **What they do:** Sensor-agnostic, no-code platform combining predictive maintenance + full CMMS in a single pane. Positioned specifically for mid-sized brownfield manufacturers. Claims deployment in under 14 days. Value-based pricing per critical asset.
- **Why they matter:** Nearly identical buyer segment to FactoryLM (mid-market, brownfield). Strong in content marketing and positioning.
- **Key gap vs. FactoryLM:** No documentation ingestion or PLC tag → asset name mapping. Sensor-based signal intelligence, not knowledge-base grounded answers.

---

**4. AssetWatch**
- **Website:** [assetwatch.com](https://assetwatch.com)
- **Crunchbase:** [tracxn.com/d/companies/assetwatch](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/assetwatch/__yL1qVGzmeGCqH1UltoLvjlmj_ro0BFimJEqfw9SO0Bc)
- **What they do:** "Hardware-plus-service" predictive maintenance with a Human-in-the-Loop model. Wireless tri-axial vibration and temperature sensors + remote monitoring by certified vibration analysts. Inc. 5000 #526 in 2025 (second time on list). Fast-growing.
- **Why they matter:** Fastest-growing in the turnkey PdM segment. Competes on the trust of having humans validate AI alerts — similar to how FactoryLM grounds answers in docs.
- **Key gap:** No knowledge management layer. No CMMS. Sensor + analyst service only.

---

**5. SparkCognition**
- **Website:** [sparkcognition.com](https://www.sparkcognition.com)
- **What they do:** Austin-based enterprise industrial AI. Darwin AI platform builds predictive maintenance models from industrial data without requiring dedicated data science teams. Addresses skills gap for mid-market manufacturers.
- **Customers:** Major oil & gas, utilities, aerospace, manufacturing.
- **Why they matter:** Known for removing data science barrier to entry — similar democratization angle to FactoryLM's "no AI expertise required" positioning.
- **Key gap:** Enterprise-only pricing. No field technician UX. No document/manual namespace.

---

### CATEGORY 2 — CMMS with AI Copilot

---

**6. MaintainX**
- **Website:** [getmaintainx.com](https://www.getmaintainx.com)
- **Crunchbase:** [crunchbase.com/organization/maintainx](https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/maintainx)
- **What they do:** AI-powered CMMS and EAM platform. Launched MaintainX CoPilot AI (July 2025) alongside $150M Series D. Features: natural language reporting, failure prediction, parts demand forecasting, auto-generate procedures from manuals or photos, summarize work orders.
- **Scale:** $2.5B valuation (July 2025). #1 CMMS on G2 Winter 2026. Forbes 2025 Cloud 100.
- **Why they matter:** The best-funded direct CMMS competitor. AI Copilot now does some of what MIRA does (manual procedure generation). Watch for them moving into documentation namespace.
- **Key gap vs. FactoryLM:** Work-order-centric. No UNS/PLC tag reconciliation. No OEM documentation index. Copilot is assistive, not grounded in plant-specific namespaces.

---

**7. Limble CMMS**
- **Website:** [limble.com](https://limble.com)
- **What they do:** CMMS with AI PM Builder (available from Standard plan, $28/user). Positioned as the most AI-accessible CMMS at lower tiers. Strong mid-market presence.
- **Why they matter:** Competitive pricing + AI PM Builder makes them accessible to exactly your target customer. 2026 comparison articles regularly list Limble vs. MaintainX.
- **Key gap:** No predictive intelligence. No equipment documentation namespace. Maintenance scheduling only.

---

**8. UpKeep**
- **Website:** [upkeep.com](https://upkeep.com)
- **What they do:** Mobile-first CMMS + asset lifecycle management. Integrates IoT sensor hardware. Strong mobile UX for field technicians. EAM-grade for mid-to-large facilities.
- **Why they matter:** Strong in asset lifecycle tracking — the "where is the asset in its life" view. Technician mobile experience overlaps with MIRA's floor-level UX goal.
- **Key gap:** No AI diagnostic answers grounded in manuals. No namespace structuring.

---

**9. Fiix (Rockwell Automation)**
- **Website:** [fiix.io](https://www.fiix.io)
- **What they do:** AI-powered CMMS, acquired by Rockwell Automation (2020). Fiix Foresight predictive maintenance (Professional+ tier, $75/user). Best suited for Rockwell/Allen-Bradley automation environments.
- **Why they matter:** Rockwell distribution + deep integration into Allen-Bradley PLCs = strong footing with exactly the customers who need PLC tag reconciliation. Potential channel threat.
- **Key gap:** Locked into Rockwell ecosystem. No cross-vendor OEM documentation namespace. Foresight requires Professional tier.

---

**10. IBM Maximo Application Suite**
- **Website:** [ibm.com/products/maximo](https://www.ibm.com/products/maximo)
- **What they do:** Enterprise EAM platform. Maximo 9.1 (June 2025) launched with AI agents — "a fundamental reimagining of how organizations converse with their physical assets." Agentic AI for maintenance, inspection, and operational planning.
- **Why they matter:** The legacy enterprise EAM standard. Now aggressively adding AI agents. Their customers are the large plants FactoryLM could eventually grow into.
- **Key gap:** Heavy enterprise implementation (months/years). No SMB path. No brownfield namespace structuring service.

---

### CATEGORY 3 — Industrial AI / Asset Performance Management

---

**11. C3.ai**
- **Website:** [c3.ai](https://c3.ai)
- **What they do:** Enterprise AI application platform. C3 AI Reliability for predictive maintenance. Named a Leader in Verdantix Industrial AI Analytics 2025. Contracts: U.S. Air Force PANDA predictive maintenance ($450M ceiling), Nucor Steel (enterprise-wide AI), Baker Hughes (oil & gas).
- **Why they matter:** The enterprise AI benchmark for large industrial deployments. Strong government and energy sector credibility.
- **Key gap:** No SMB/mid-market path. Requires large IT organizations to deploy. Not a field-technician tool.

---

**12. AVEVA (Schneider Electric)**
- **Website:** [aveva.com](https://www.aveva.com)
- **What they do:** Industrial intelligence platform. Now part of Schneider Electric. Developed Industrial AI Assistant on Microsoft Azure (conversational interface for operational data). April 2025 partnership with ServiceNow for unified IT/OT management via CONNECT platform.
- **Why they matter:** The IT/OT convergence play. Their CONNECT platform is trying to bridge the gap FactoryLM also addresses (IT data + OT data in one place). Heavy enterprise.
- **Key gap:** No SMB play. No manual namespace for technicians. OT data aggregation, not maintenance intelligence grounded in documentation.

---

**13. AspenTech (Emerson)**
- **Website:** [aspentech.com](https://www.aspentech.com)
- **What they do:** Software for asset optimization and operational excellence in process industries (oil & gas, chemicals, pharma). Aspen Mtell uses ML to predict equipment failures weeks in advance — noted as "most advanced ML for predicting failures" in PdM space. Now part of Emerson.
- **Why they matter:** Dominant in process industries. Months-ahead failure prediction is their key differentiator.
- **Key gap:** Process industry only. No manufacturing floor / technician tooling. No documentation namespace.

---

**14. Prometheus Group**
- **Website:** [prometheusgroup.com](https://www.prometheusgroup.com)
- **What they do:** EAM software integrating with SAP, IBM Maximo, and Oracle. Founded 1998. Specializes in planning, scheduling, and turnaround management for asset-intensive organizations. AI Platform eliminates silos and provides single source of truth.
- **Why they matter:** They operate as a layer on top of SAP/Maximo — similar to how FactoryLM operates as a layer on top of existing plant infrastructure, adding intelligence.
- **Key gap:** No AI diagnostic answers. Planning/scheduling focus. Requires existing SAP/Maximo licenses.

---

**15. Augmentir**
- **Website:** [augmentir.com](https://www.augmentir.com)
- **Verdantix report:** [verdantix.com — 10 GenAI Industrial Copilot Vendors 2025](https://www.verdantix.com/venture/report/market-insight-10-genai-powered-industrial-copilot-vendors-to-watch-in-2025)
- **What they do:** Connected worker platform with AI-powered work instructions and industrial copilot. GenAI for frontline workers — step-by-step guidance, skills management, remote assistance. Listed in Verdantix's "10 GenAI Industrial Copilot Vendors to Watch in 2025."
- **Why they matter:** "Industrial Copilot" positioning overlaps with MIRA. Focus on frontline worker experience.
- **Key gap:** Work instructions and training, not equipment diagnostic intelligence. No OEM manual namespace.

---

### CATEGORY 4 — Frontline Operations / Manufacturing Execution

---

**16. Tulip**
- **Website:** [tulip.co](https://tulip.co)
- **What they do:** No-code frontline operations platform. 43,000+ apps supporting 60,000 frontline workers across 1,000+ customer sites (as of 2025). $120M Series D (Jan 2026, $1.3B valuation). Mitsubishi Electric strategic investor. Generative AI adoption grew 364% in 2 years. Strong in pharma, biotech, medical device.
- **Why they matter:** Fastest-growing frontline operations platform. Their AI automation and co-authoring features are expanding into diagnosis territory. Mitsubishi partnership could bring them into heavy equipment.
- **Key gap:** Process and workflow capture, not equipment diagnostic intelligence. No PLC tag or OEM manual integration.

---

**17. Sight Machine**
- **Website:** [sightmachine.com](https://www.sightmachine.com)
- **CB Insights:** [cbinsights.com/compare/sight-machine-vs-tulip-interfaces](https://www.cbinsights.com/compare/sight-machine-vs-tulip-interfaces)
- **What they do:** Manufacturing data platform — connects, unifies, and analyzes factory data. Microsoft partnership (featured at Hannover Messe 2025 for real-time factory visibility). Focuses on production analytics and continuous improvement.
- **Why they matter:** They sit between the OT data layer and business intelligence — similar to FactoryLM's UNS bridge role but focused on production, not maintenance.
- **Key gap:** Production analytics focus, not maintenance intelligence. No manual documentation namespace.

---

**18. Facilio**
- **Website:** [facilio.com](https://facilio.com)
- **What they do:** Connected buildings and facilities management platform. FM Copilot allows frontline workers to query SOPs ("What's the SOP for replacing a chiller valve?") and get step-by-step answers with historical records. Strong in commercial real estate and facilities management, expanding to industrial.
- **Why they matter:** Their FM Copilot is the closest product analog to MIRA — knowledge-grounded Q&A for maintenance technicians. Different vertical (facilities vs. manufacturing) but same UX concept.
- **Key gap:** Facilities focus (HVAC, building systems), not manufacturing floor. No PLC integration.

---

### CATEGORY 5 — Condition Monitoring / Predictive Maintenance Hardware+AI

---

**19. Fluke Reliability / Azima DLI**
- **Website:** [reliability.fluke.com](https://reliability.fluke.com) | [azimadli.com](https://www.azimadli.com)
- **What they do:** Fluke Reliability acquired Azima DLI (Aug 2023). Azima AI trained on 30 years of vibration data, 100 trillion data points. 93% of machine tests analyzed without human intervention. Integrates with eMaint CMMS. Fluke is the trusted tool brand on every maintenance tech's belt.
- **Why they matter:** Brand trust on the floor is unmatched. Combining AI-driven vibration analysis with the Fluke brand could accelerate adoption of AI diagnostics at the technician level.
- **Key gap:** Vibration/condition monitoring only. No documentation namespace. eMaint CMMS is separate product.

---

**20. Samsara**
- **Website:** [samsara.com](https://www.samsara.com)
- **What they do:** Connected operations platform for industrial fleets and facilities. IoT sensors, video-based AI, and real-time data for equipment monitoring. Started in fleet, expanding to industrial operations.
- **Why they matter:** Massive capital base and IoT infrastructure. If they push deeper into plant maintenance AI (beyond fleet), they become a significant threat through IoT-first sensor deployment.
- **Key gap:** Fleet-first, not plant maintenance. No documentation or knowledge management layer.

---

**21. SKF Rotating Equipment Performance**
- **Website:** [skf.com](https://www.skf.com/us/products/condition-monitoring)
- **What they do:** Global bearing and mechanical components manufacturer adding industrial AI. IMx remote condition monitoring, online vibration analysis, predictive maintenance services. Deep OEM knowledge of rotating equipment failure modes.
- **Why they matter:** OEM credibility — they made the bearings, they know how they fail. Their AI is trained on decades of rotating equipment data from their own manufactured components.
- **Key gap:** Rotating equipment only. OEM-specific bias. No multi-vendor plant intelligence.

---

### CATEGORY 6 — Crane / Heavy Equipment Specific

---

**22. Konecranes**
- **Website:** [konecranes.com](https://www.konecranes.com)
- **What they do:** Global crane OEM and service company. MAINMAN® maintenance software, TRUCONNECT remote condition monitoring (sensors + AI alerts), predictive maintenance programs, and a decade of investment in data-driven crane service. AI/ML from their Lyon Data Science Lab.
- **Why they matter (CraneSync):** The primary incumbent in intelligent crane maintenance. They own the hardware, the service contracts, and are building the AI. CraneSync must differentiate on speed, cost, and OEM-agnostic coverage.
- **Key gap vs. CraneSync:** Proprietary/closed ecosystem (Konecranes cranes only). Service contracts are expensive. Not designed for third-party crane service companies.

---

**23. Averroes.ai**
- **Website:** [averroes.ai](https://averroes.ai)
- **Blog on crane software:** [averroes.ai/blog/crane-inspection-software](https://averroes.ai/blog/crane-inspection-software)
- **What they do:** AI-powered inspection software for cranes and industrial equipment. Appeared in 2025 lists of top crane inspection software. Computer vision and AI for inspection documentation and compliance.
- **Why they matter (CraneSync):** Emerging AI-native player in crane inspection — same initial beachhead as CraneSync. One to watch/partner with.

---

**24. Columbus McKinnon (CMCO)**
- **Website:** [cmco.com](https://www.cmco.com)
- **What they do:** Manufacturer of hoists, rigging, and lifting equipment. Starting to add digital/IoT monitoring to products (load cells, smart hoists). Large installed base of overhead cranes and lifting equipment in North American manufacturing.
- **Why they matter (CraneSync):** Hardware incumbent with vast installed base of cranes and hoists that are currently unmonitored. CraneSync could be their AI service layer.
- **Key gap:** Hardware manufacturer, not software/AI company. Service intelligence is nascent.

---

### CATEGORY 7 — Industrial Knowledge / OT Platform

---

**25. Inductive Automation (Ignition)**
- **Website:** [inductiveautomation.com](https://inductiveautomation.com)
- **What they do:** The standard SCADA/HMI platform across North American manufacturing. Ignition is deployed at thousands of plants. 2025 ICC: announced MCP Module for 2026 and LLM integration ("Hello AI, Meet Ignition"). Exploring agent-based AI inside the Ignition ecosystem.
- **Why they matter:** This is a platform you already integrate with (MIRA's Ignition cloud-chat). They're building toward the same namespace-aware AI assistant you've already built. Either a competitor, a channel partner, or both.
- **Key gap:** They're a platform company, not a maintenance intelligence company. Building AI on Ignition requires the structuring work FactoryLM already does. FactoryLM is the "what to answer" layer; Ignition is the "where the data lives" layer.

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **Most-watched threats to FactoryLM:** MaintainX (AI Copilot expanding into procedure generation), Tractian (full-stack + same buyer), Fiix/Rockwell (Allen-Bradley installed base), and Augury (enterprise benchmark setting buyer expectations).

2. **FactoryLM's differentiated moat:** No competitor combines documentation namespace + PLC tag reconciliation + tribal knowledge capture + MIRA diagnostic AI at SMB/mid-market pricing. The $500 assessment + namespace structuring service is uniquely positioned.

3. **Crane (CraneSync) primary threat:** Konecranes is the incumbent. CraneSync's differentiation must be OEM-agnostic coverage, third-party service company enablement, and faster/cheaper deployment than Konecranes' proprietary ecosystem.

4. **Whitespace opportunity:** Nobody is building the "maintenance namespace structuring as a service" for brownfield plants below enterprise scale. FactoryLM owns this gap. Protect it.
